senses.gloss: The eleventh letter of the Arabic alphabet, written in the Arabic script; preceded by ر (r) and followed by س (s).
The seventh numeral symbol of the Arabic alphabet (traditional abjad order, used for list numbering), written in the Arabic script; preceded by و (w) and followed by ح (ḥ).

etymology.text: From the Nabataean letter 𐢉 (l, “zayin”), derived from the Phoenician letter 𐤆 (l, “zayin”), from an uncertain Egyptian hieroglyph (𓈔?,𓏭?). See also Classical Syriac ܙ (l, “zayin”), Hebrew ז (l, “zayin”), Ancient Greek ζ (z), Latin Z.
